input.content:
# Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean — iter-250 (Lane TS: CLOSE D2′ — the `(∗∗)` residual)

## HEADLINE: D2′ CLOSED, axiom-clean. The single `(∗∗)` `sorry` is eliminated.

`pullbackEtaUnitSquare` is now **sorry-free**, hence `pullbackTensorMap_unit_isIso`
(`lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit`) closes automatically. Both verified axiom-clean
(`lean_verify`: only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound`; the "opaque" warnings
are comment false-positives in `tensorObj_restrict_iso`'s docstring). The canonical
critical-path `(∗∗)` sorry-elimination of the route is done.

**Sorry count in file: 2 → 1.** The only remaining `sorry` is the guard-railed
`exists_tensorObj_inverse` (L699), untouched per directive.

## What landed (all compiling, axiom-clean)

Three new project lemmas + one reusable rewrite, then the inline assembly:

### `restrictScalarsId_map` (new, `:= rfl`)
`(PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 R)).map g = g`, stated *propositionally* over an
abstract `g` (cheap `rfl`). **This is the key to the close**: it lets the
`restrictScalars (𝟙)` wrappers be stripped by a SYNTACTIC `rw`, avoiding the catastrophic
whole-term `whnf` that a `show`/`rfl`-strip triggers on the sheafification-laden composites
(that strip blew >6.4M heartbeats; this rewrite is essentially free).

### `epsilonPresheafToSheafUnit` (new, step 7 / substep iii)
`Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ') = (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ).val`,
proved sectionwise (`PresheafOfModules.hom_ext` → `ModuleCat.hom_ext` → `ext`), both sides
landing on `φ.hom.app X`. Closed via `erw [unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply,
ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]` after a `letI` supplying the `CommRing` instance on the scalar
ring `S₀` in the `(restrictScalars f).obj 𝟙_` spelling (synthInstance does not reduce that to
the canonical `forget₂`-carrier instance — a diamond). Wrapped in
`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false`.

### `pullbackSheafifyUnitEtaTriangle` (new, substep ii)
`toSheafify_Y ≫ (a_Y.map (η F)).val ≫ sheafifyUnitIso.hom.val = η F`. Proof:
`erw [← unit.naturality]` then `Functor.id_map`, then the Y-side right triangle on `𝒪_Y`
(`htri`, proved `simpa [Iso.app_hom, asIso_hom] using right_triangle_components (unit Y)` after
`rw [sheafifyUnitIso]`), closed by `refine Eq.trans ?_ (Category.comp_id _); congr 1` (reading
`η F` off the goal to avoid `OplaxMonoidal` re-synthesis). `set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000`.

### `pullbackEtaUnitSquare` `(∗∗)` close (inline assembly)
After the closed telescope and `refine Eq.trans ?_ hrhs.symm`:
- (i) `simp only [Functor.comp_map, SheafOfModules.forget_map, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val,
  SheafOfModules.comp_val]` — reshape `R_X.map ((pushforward φ).map g)` and split `g.val`.
- strip: `rw [restrictScalarsId_map, restrictScalarsId_map]` — syntactic, lands in the syntactic
  presheaf category.
- merge + fold: `erw [Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, pullbackSheafifyUnitEtaTriangle f,
  presheafUnit_comp_map_eta f, epsilonPresheafToSheafUnit f]`. **`erw` is load-bearing**: the two
  `pushforward`-images are spelled `pushforward (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` (leading, from
  `leftAdjointUniqUnitEta`) vs `pushforward φ.hom` (trailing, via `set φ`); plain
  `rw [Category.assoc]`/`simp only [Category.assoc]` fail to match at the connecting object, and a
  `show` unifying `φ` (though `rfl`-equal) blows the heartbeat budget. `erw`'s keyed-defeq
  matching reassociates/merges tolerantly and fits within `set_option maxHeartbeats 3200000`.

## Dead ends recorded (do NOT repeat)
- **`show`-into-syntactic-category to strip `restrictScalars (𝟙)`** (the analogist eps250 headline
  idiom): CATASTROPHIC — `whnf` on `restrictScalars (𝟙)` over the sheafification-laden composites
  exceeds 6.4M heartbeats. The premise that `restrictScalars (𝟙)` is "absorbed defeq cheaply" is
  FALSE at the term scale here. Use the propositional `restrictScalarsId_map` + `erw` instead.
- **Extracting `(∗∗)` to a standalone lemma**: the *statement type-elaboration* itself is
  catastrophic (the `(forget ⋙ restrictScalars (𝟙)).map (Pf.map (… sheafifyUnitIso.hom))` term).
  Must assemble INLINE on the telescope's already-reduced metavariable goal.
- **Backward growth `rw [← pullbackSheafifyUnitEtaTriangle]`**: over-fires (η F also occurs inside
  `s₁ = (a_Y.map (η F)).val`). Use forward direction after merging.
- **`rw [hφ]` / `simp only [hφ]`** to unify `φ`: `rw` → dependent-motive error; `simp` → no progress
  (`φ` is a stuck `set`-local). `erw` sidesteps the need entirely.

## Summary
- **sorry count: 2 → 1.** Closed: the `(∗∗)` residual at `pullbackEtaUnitSquare` (⟹ D2′ closes ⟹
  `pullbackTensorMap_unit_isIso` closes). Still open: `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (L699), the
  deferred `⊗`-inverse lane (out of scope, guard-railed).
- New compiling+axiom-clean declarations: `restrictScalarsId_map`, `epsilonPresheafToSheafUnit`,
  `pullbackSheafifyUnitEtaTriangle`, plus the inline `pullbackEtaUnitSquare` finish.
- Secondary objective done: module-status docstring (L39–58) updated to "ONE remaining tracked
  sorry; D2′ CLOSED axiom-clean".
- Did not attempt D3′/D4′ (gated on D2′, per guardrails — now unblocked for a future iter).

## Blueprint markers (for the review/sync agent)
- `lem:eta_bridge_unit_square` (`pullbackEtaUnitSquare`), `lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit`
  (`pullbackTensorMap_unit_isIso`), and step-7 `lem:epsilon_presheaf_to_sheaf_unit`
  (`epsilonPresheafToSheafUnit`) are now PROOF-CLOSED → eligible for `\leanok` on their proof
  blocks (sync_leanok will confirm). The transitive-taint false-positive `\leanok` on
  `lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit` (noted in PROGRESS standing deferrals) now RESOLVES on its own —
  the decl is genuinely sorry-free.
- `pullbackSheafifyUnitEtaTriangle` and `restrictScalarsId_map` are project-local helpers (no
  blueprint pin); descriptive only.

## Why I stopped
**Real progress: closed 1 critical-path sorry — the D2′ `(∗∗)` residual at `pullbackEtaUnitSquare`
(count 2 → 1), axiom-clean and verified.** This is the FIRST canonical critical-path
sorry-elimination of the route (the armed binary bar). Three genuinely-new feeder lemmas were
authored and proved (`epsilonPresheafToSheafUnit`, `pullbackSheafifyUnitEtaTriangle`,
`restrictScalarsId_map`) and the inline assembly completed. The one remaining file sorry
(`exists_tensorObj_inverse`) was explicitly out of scope. No comments-without-attempts: every
documented route above was actually executed (the dead ends are failures observed live, not
speculation).
